title: archidendron hispidum
intro: Archidendron hispidum, also known as the prickly jack tree, is a species of tree in the family Fabaceae. It is native to the tropical regions of Asia and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Archidendron hispidum include treating fever, diarrhea, and respiratory issues, as well as being used as an antiseptic and astringent. In herbal preparations, the leaves and bark of the plant are often used to make teas, infusions, and ointments, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential uses and effects.
